      "url": "https://www.parliament.lk/en/members-of-parliament/role-of-mps",
      "title": "Parliament of Sri Lanka - Role of Members of Parliament",
      "content": "##### Members of Parliament. Role of Members of Parliament. ##### Business of Parliament. ##### My Parliament. # Role of Members of Parliament. 3. Role of Members of Parliament. The Role of a Member of Parliament (MP) in Sri Lanka involves many different tasks and responsibilities referred above that are closely connected to the country's democracy. Aside from representing the people, MPs also have a crucial role in making laws. Participating actively in parliamentary committees is an important part of their job. In these committees, MPs study specific areas of governance, investigate important matters, and come up with suggestions on how to deal with urgent issues facing the country. MPs have a strong commitment to serving the best interests of the people and the country. It represents a special responsibility and a serious commitment to serving the people, upholding democratic values, principals and leading the country towards progress and success. ##### **Members of Parliament**. Role of Members of Parliament. ##### **Business of Parliament**.",

Engage with your Parliament by logging in. # Evolution of Parliament. This building was declared open on January 29, 1930 by the then Governor Sir Herbert Stanley and housed the legislature till it was shifted to the new Parliamentary Complex at Sri Jayewardenepura Kotte in April 1982. The Parliament consisted of the Queen (represented by the Governor - General) and two Houses, namely the Senate and the House of Representatives. * The Parliament 1978 - todate - (225 Members).
